Why Nigeria — Why Mecarun — Why Now
⛽

Fuel Subsidy Removal — Every Fleet Manager Is Under Existential Pressure

Diesel hit ₦1,000+/L after subsidy removal. A logistics truck now spends ₦7.5M/month on fuel. C99's 12% fuel economy is an immediate, verifiable rescue package — not a promise, a calculation any fleet manager can validate within 30 days. This is survival, not optimisation.

🏭

Dangote Refinery — Euro V Fuel Creates Perfect Catalytic Conditions

Dangote's Lekki refinery now produces Euro V-specification fuel. High-quality base fuel + Mecarun catalytic additives = maximum efficiency. Fleet operators who adopt C99 with Euro V fuel see compounding returns no competitor additive can match.

🌡️

Lagos Destroys Standard Products — Mecarun Was Built for This Climate

35–42°C ambient temperature. 85–95% coastal humidity. 12 km/h average traffic speed. ABRO and STP were never formulated for these extremes. P18's electrochemical metal bonding activates at engine-off — the only product in West Africa that protects during cold starts, when 90% of engine wear occurs.

🚢

Apapa Port & Industrial Sector — NATO-Grade Protection at Scale

Apapa and Tincan ports handle $50B+ in cargo. Equipment corrosion costs ₦2.8B/year in avoidable maintenance. WPM holds NATO C-634 certification — the only civilian protectant at this standard in all of West Africa.

🇫🇷

Made in France — A Moat No Competitor Can Copy

Nigerian enterprise buyers trust French technical products in a way they never will trust unbranded American alternatives. "Made in France, exclusive to Nigeria by Control Buy System Ltd." is a defensible competitive moat backed by ISO 9001 certification and 30+ years of European field use.

Market Context

Nigeria's Fuel Bill Is a Revenue Opportunity

Generator Sector
622,000+

generators running 24/7 across telco towers, hotels, hospitals and factories

Current Diesel Price
₦1,000+

per litre — up from ₦185 pre-subsidy removal. Existential pressure on every fleet

The removal of fuel subsidies has fundamentally changed the economics of every Nigerian fleet operator. A logistics truck doing the Lagos–Kano route now spends ₦7.5M per month on fuel alone. Hotel owners running 8 generators watch ₦1.4M per generator disappear monthly into diesel costs. These operators are not looking for a better product — they are looking for a rescue package. C99 is that rescue package, with a verified 12% fuel economy reduction that pays for itself within the first tank fill-up.

Scientific Proof

How C99 Works at the Molecular Level

C99 deploys a proprietary organo-metallic catalyst that lowers the fuel's activation energy threshold, enabling complete combustion at lower temperatures. More energy extracted from the same litre of fuel. Less unburnt hydrocarbons. Dramatically reduced injector fouling. Secondary: a biocide chemistry eliminates Pseudomonas aeruginosa and algae colonies endemic to Nigerian underground fuel tanks.

Fuel atomisation +23% — Finer droplets → larger surface area → more complete combustion per gram of fuel

Cetane number boost — Equivalent to upgrading diesel grade. Critical for Tokunbo engines with worn injection timing

Carbon deposit inhibition — Prevents injector fouling over 10,000+ km cycles, eliminating ₦45,000–₦85,000 per-injector replacements

Biocide action — Eliminates microbial contamination in fuel tanks. Dirty, bacteria-laden fuel destroys injection pumps silently.

Market Context

Lagos Kills Engines. P18 Doesn't Let It.

Lagos Traffic Speed
12 km/h

average speed. Engines idle 4–6h daily. Maximum thermal stress, zero oil film renewal.

Engine Replacement
₦450k–₦2M

P18 treatment cost: ₦15,000–₦25,000 per oil change. Minimum 30× ROI.

35–42°C ambient temperature accelerates oil viscosity breakdown by 300% vs European conditions. Nigerian vehicles — predominantly Tokunbo imports averaging 15+ years old — arrive with worn cylinders and degraded seals. Every cold start destroys metal surfaces. P18's electrochemical bonding chemistry forms a molecular-level protective layer that persists even when the engine is off. It is the only additive on the Nigerian market that eliminates cold-start wear — which accounts for 90% of total engine wear over a vehicle's lifetime.

Scientific Proof

Electrochemical Metal Bonding Technology

P18 deploys synthetic meta-lubricant molecules that form an electrochemical bond with ferrous metal surfaces under pressure and heat. Unlike MoS2 or PTFE particles — which physically coat surfaces and clog oil filters — P18 modifies the metal surface crystallography itself, creating a permanently low-friction surface that outperforms any particle-based competitor.

Zero solid particles — No filter clogging, no sedimentation in oil galleries, no premature oil pump wear

Active at engine-off — The bonded layer protects during cold starts. 90% of engine wear occurs in the first 30 seconds after ignition.

Viscosity stabilisation at 120°C — Maintains SAE grade performance under Lagos traffic thermal stress conditions

Universal compatibility — Works with all engine oils, gearbox fluids, industrial machinery lubricants

Market Context

87% of Vehicles Are Pre-Fouled at Import

Workshop Decarbonisation
₦180k–₦350k

3–5 days. Engine disassembly. Business downtime. Risk of damage.

ECO 10000 Treatment
₦18,000

30 minutes. Vehicle stays on the road. No tools. No disassembly. Same result.

87% of Nigerian vehicles are imported used ('Tokunbo') from Japan and Europe. Average carbon accumulation at entry equals 120,000+ km of deposits. The VIO now enforces emission tests nationally — and 40% of tested vehicles currently fail due to carbon-related issues. This creates a legal mandate for ECO 10000. It is not a luxury product. It is compliance chemistry. 920,000 vehicles failing VIO tests every year is a captive market.

Scientific Proof

Solvent-Phase Carbon Dissolution Chemistry

ECO 10000 uses a proprietary ester-based solvent system with exceptional carbon-affinity coefficients. Under injection system pressure, it attacks polyaromatic hydrocarbon bonds in carbon deposits (calamine), dissolving them without mechanical action. It also scavenges free water — absorbing 15% of its volume — preventing injector corrosion endemic in Lagos and Port Harcourt's coastal humidity.

400:1 carbon dissolution — Dissolves 400× its weight in polyaromatic carbon, calamine, varnish and oxidised oil residue

Water scavenging — Absorbs 15% volume in free water, preventing injector corrosion — a critical failure in Nigeria's humid coastal climate

Pressure-activated, zero disassembly — Works under injection system operating pressure. No specialist tools. No workshop. No downtime.

DPF/FAP compatible — Does not damage particulate filters. Safe on all modern emission control systems.

Market Context

The Coastal Economy Corrodes Everything. WPM Doesn't.

Coastal Humidity
85–95%

Annual relative humidity. Metal corrosion rate 6× faster than inland environments.

NPA Corrosion Cost
₦2.8B

Estimated annual port equipment maintenance caused by corrosion. 340+ cranes affected.

Lagos and Port Harcourt sit on the Atlantic coast at 85–95% annual humidity. Every piece of metal equipment faces constant chemical attack. Port infrastructure, oil equipment, motorcycles, vehicles parked in coastal areas. WPM is the only civilian product in all of West Africa to hold NATO C-634 certification — with 1,200-hour ASTM B117 salt spray resistance. Nigeria is spending billions on reactive maintenance. WPM converts that into proactive prevention at a fraction of the cost.

Scientific Proof

Penetrating Surfactant Film Technology

WPM uses a military-derived penetrating surfactant system that exploits capillary action to infiltrate micro-crevices, threaded joints and cable interstices inaccessible to standard lubricants. Volatile carriers evaporate on contact, depositing a dry, non-conductive film enriched with P18 anti-wear compounds.

Capillary penetration <0.5 micron — Infiltrates corrosion at source, not just surface. Reaches inside threaded joints, cable cores and micro-pitting

Dry film — no dust adhesion — Unlike WD-40's oil film that collects Lagos roadside dust, WPM leaves a clean, dry non-attracting residue

Dielectric — electronics safe — Non-conductive. Safe on alternators, starter motors, ECU connectors, PCBs and all vehicle electronics

P18-enriched formula — Contains the same electrochemical anti-wear agent as P18, providing active surface protection on all moving parts

Deployment Timeline — Signature to Full Revenue
Day 1 — Avenant Signed

Exclusive Territory Secured Immediately

Exclusivity takes effect upon signature. No competitor can enter Nigeria's Mecarun market from this moment. NAFDAC application initiated simultaneously.

Month 1–3 — Prospection Phase

Network Building & Demo Deployments

Lagos network activated. First demonstrations to generator fleet operators, hotel groups and automotive workshops. WhatsApp campaigns launched with video proof-of-concept content.

Month 4–6 — First Commercial Contracts

Priority Client Pipeline Signed

First contracts with top 5 clients (telecom, logistics, hospitality). NAFDAC registration progressing. Pre-approval commercial sales to established clients.

Month 7–12 — NAFDAC Clearance

Full Market Access — Year 1 Clock Starts

Certification received. Products legally cleared for full Nigerian distribution. Year 1 target (€75,000 / ₦125M) officially begins. Fleet contracts convert to long-term supply agreements.

Month 25–36 — Year 2 & Beyond

₦416M Annual Revenue at Year 3

Reorder economics activate. New sectors added (mining, aviation, marine). Year 3 target: €250,000 — the full value of exclusive Nigerian distribution.
